other_observers: 4 participants on Victor Emanuel Nature Tours tour; yes; Later that afternoon the bird was observed by Tony Leukering, Dwayne Nelson, and Brandon Percival
specific_location: Two Buttes Reservoir, from the dam to about 0.5 mi below dam
description: Large, chesty, broad-winged, hawk. Flight style and shape recalled Black Vulture. Wings short, reaching only to base of tail (about 1/3 of way to tip). Primary extension short (much shorter than on Zone-tailed Hawk), but fairly long for a Black-Hawk (longer than on Great Black-Hawk). Slaty-black overall, perhaps paler on upperparts than below. Eye dark. Bill dark, with yellow cere and some grayish bare skin in lores. Legs yellow. Tail black with prominent, band about 2" in width, centered on tail. Also, thin, crisp, white terminal band on tail tip. Underwings with some faint barring on rmiges, contrasting with dark wing linings and indistinct darker trailing edge. Ragged bulge to rear of wing indicated molt, which may suggest as third-year (TY) bird. See attached field notes and photos.Behaviors: Soaring over canyon and perched in willows along stream (twice). On most occasions it soared with Turkey Vultures. See attached field notes and photos.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
